creator: destination_wellknown
caption:
Don’t start your London trip by feeling ripped off after standing in a queue to buy a madly expensive train ticket 😬

Instead, as soon as you’ve booked your flight to Stansted Airport, head over to Stanstedexpress.com and see if you can get their ‘Early Advance Fare’. If you buy your ticket on the day, it’ll cost £23, which is absolutely insane, but if you book well in advance, the price can be as low as £9.90. The tickets can be used on all trains on the date you’ve chosen.

You can also take the bus from Stansted Airport to London. National Express has over 100 buses daily with plenty of different stops in London (for example, Victoria Station, Baker Street, Paddington, and Waterloo St). Their tickets start from £11 if you book in advance (I think they cost £17 on the day).

The massive downside of the bus is that it can take over 1.5 hours to reach a stop like Baker Street in Central London.

So I always recommend booking the train ticket ASAP 🚆

#london #stanstedairport #traveltips

transcription.text: I'm really sorry if you're flying into London's Stansted Airport, but here is the number one thing you need to do to save time and money before you fly to Stansted. By the way, hi, I'm Chris. I used to run a travel agency, but nowadays I just share all my best travel tips on this little app. Stansted Airport is located over 40 miles north of London, so getting into town takes ages. The fastest option is the Stansted Express train, and that will take you down to London in about 50 minutes. The tickets cost a staggering £23 if you buy them in the ticket machines, where there's usually a long queue. But if you make sure to buy your tickets well in advance, you can actually get them for as little as £9.90. So make sure you book your train tickets as soon as you've booked your flight into London's Stansted Airport.
